channel.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "[Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora." - Excuse me, but can somebody give me crits or greenlight on this thread?

item.title: Re: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback. And no, he doesn't look like The Mask at all. The trickster isn't actually acting in ways like a clown or a cartoon character. The trickster I'm describing isn't also chaotic but enjoys joking and helping others at the same time shown to be always carefree, immature, goofy, unpredictable, and comes off as a slight irresponsible idiot. But sometimes, the character can be serious in some situations. And I didn't say he's energetic nor burst into happiness. He's just enjoying his days like a regular person with nothing to do except for good old fashion walking, hanging out.

item.title: Re: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded:
My concern is that this feels too narratively close to the mask still; like the effects are clearly based off that; but so is a lot of the character and conflict here too, even if the actual way this plays out is quite different. What I mean is that Michael is basically a straight rip of Jim Carrey's character, with like, the same 'wants to help but is chaotic' vibe as the mask has already and it feels…very derivative.

Beyond this, I don't actually have a good idea of how this works: you mention the big twist is that michael is actually real sad and that's not an awful idea but I don't really get how you're like, intending building up to it? And I'm really not convinced it's enough of a twist to carry this: it kinda feels like it'd be better being very upfront with it and then really exploring like, the dichotomy of michael wanting everyone to be happy and like trying to help and him just being super depressed and hating life.

I also don't really have a good idea of what the core plot beats you want to hit are: there's like, an obvious opening where you introduce his powers, and then…I have no idea? You refer to interviews and some logs but it feels like they're expository regarding his powers over like, actually telling a story and imo that will make them really boring to read.

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Humor/Emotional

Page Layout: Containment procedure, description of the entity, interview, and some logs containing hints of the entity's personality and how it's powers work.

Elevator Pitch: A reality-altering pitched black humanoid-entity with a menacing appearance, who grins and smiles endlessly, dressed up in a mafia's suit of the late 19s, however, with the mind of a "helpful scapegoat" and the soul of a benevolent trickster who was formerly a man named Michael Malloy.

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

After attempting to persuade a female researcher to become his girlfriend while walking her way home, he was convinced to be held in Foundation custody. Based on the logs, Michael states that he was brought back to life by a "transparent ghost" in a pitched-black place after being murdered by a group of "violent scammers" that pretends to be his friends. Michael claims that he was bestowed with a power named "Anti-God", but it is unknown of its effects to all, including Michael. The Foundation temporarily presume it is the name of the reality-bending properties.

The Foundation noted that they must never ask Michael for help in doing tasks or testings with SCPs that must be taken seriously. As he helps the Foundation, he is unaware of the outcome he may cause and makes many shenanigans on those peoples' works during the process. Michael would become panic in knowing that he made big mistakes but still tries to hide his shocked feelings and become acts carefree about it. As people complain or scold him, he instantly protests in all kinds of ways. This also includes blaming others, mocking them, saying meaningless things that draw a big confusion to the Foundation to be out of words.

The reason why Michael smile endlessly and always jokes around is that:

Michael wasn't a person that lived a good life. He never had friends, was all alone, lost his job, his father died in the war, and his mother died due to sickness. Michael doesn't want anyone to experience the same way that he had. He feared to remind of his past.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: A tragic anomaly that smiles endlessly while hiding what it feels inside of its heart. Like we said "The ones that have the brightest smiles, and the loudest laughs have the most painful past.".

Additional Notes: This SCP was inspired by The Mask (1994).
